hero.heading: WE BUILD THE MACHINE THAT RUNS YOUR BUSINESS.
hero.subtitle: Wire your inbox into your ledger. Run your whole operation on one backbone. Put your own app in your crew's hands. Fixed prices, paid at checkpoints — and 30 days to walk away.
hero.founderLine: I scaled a deck and railing business before writing OPS. SPEC starts the same way: field problems first, software second.

whiteLabel.label: // WHITE LABEL
whiteLabel.line: Your name on it. We run everything behind it.
whiteLabel.detail: Your name, your icon, your App Store listing — published and operated by OPS end to end. The store's Seller line reads OPS; everything above the fold is you. Want full ownership later? Apple's App Transfer moves it to your account, reviews intact.

ongoing.subscriptionNote: The OPS subscription is its own product. It applies only if you run OPS — and it starts free.

questionnaire.q1.question: How much do you want to change?
questionnaire.q1.configure: Configure OPS around how we already work
questionnaire.q1.one_module: Build one custom module for my trade
questionnaire.q1.rebuild: Rebuild my whole operation on OPS

questionnaire.q3.question: How big is your crew?
questionnaire.q4.question: What's your budget for the build?

questionnaire.rationale.spec02: SPEC-02 fits — one backbone for your whole operation, run for you.
questionnaire.rationale.spec03: SPEC-03 fits — a standalone tool built only for you.

guarantees.col1.title: 30 days to walk away
guarantees.col1.body: Every tier. One invocation, written notice, no defect proof required. Thirty days from delivery to decide it isn't right.
guarantees.col2.title: What happens if you do
guarantees.col2.body: SPEC-01: your money back — the automations live in your accounts and stay put. Hosted work: we switch it off and refund per the payment schedule. Days, not months.
guarantees.col3.title: No hostages
guarantees.col3.body: Your data exports clean. Your brand assets stay yours. Off-boarding is written into the terms — it's part of the deal, not a negotiation.

faq.items.question: What do the three tiers actually do?
faq.items.answer: SPEC-01 · WORKFLOWS wires together the tools you already use — up to 3 production automations in your own accounts, $2,000 fixed. SPEC-02 · SYSTEMS gives your operation one backbone — jobs, clients, and money in one system, with dashboards — $7,500 fixed. SPEC-03 · PROPRIETARY is a standalone trade tool, designed and operated by us, with your name on it if you want it — from $25,000, total locked at scope sign-off.

faq.items.question: Do I need OPS to buy this?
faq.items.answer: No. SPEC-01 runs entirely in your own accounts — your inbox, your spreadsheets, your tools. You create a free OPS account so approvals and updates have somewhere to live; no subscription required. SPEC-02 needs a subscription only if your backbone lives inside your OPS account. SPEC-03 runs on its own database either way.

faq.items.question: How do payments work?
faq.items.answer: By tier. SPEC-01 is 50/50 — $1,000 books your slot, $1,000 at the delivery walkthrough. SPEC-02 is four checkpoints of $1,875: deposit, scope sign-off, midpoint review, delivery. SPEC-03 opens with a fixed $6,250 deposit against the $25,000 floor; your total locks at scope sign-off and the remainder splits across the last three checkpoints. Everything after the deposit is invoiced net-15.

faq.items.question: What's a care plan, and why is it required?
faq.items.answer: When we operate your system, someone has to be on the hook for it. That's the care plan: $395/mo on SPEC-02, from $750/mo on SPEC-03 — monitoring, fixes, hosting where we run it, store compliance on apps, and included change-hours (2 a month on SYSTEMS, 3 on PROPRIETARY). Work beyond the included hours is $200/hr, always quoted before we start. Billing begins when your support window ends. If your SPEC-02 backbone lives in your own OPS account, care is optional after the support window.

faq.items.question: Where does my SPEC-02 backbone live?
faq.items.answer: Wherever the outcome is best. If you run OPS, we build it into your OPS account and it lives with you. If you don't, we run it standalone on infrastructure we operate — hosting is included in the care plan. Decided in discovery, in writing.

faq.items.question: What does white label mean on SPEC-03?
faq.items.answer: Your name, your icon, your App Store listing — your clients see your company end to end. +$4,000 at build, +$200/mo on care. One line of fine print we'll say out loud: Apple binds the store's Seller field to the developer account, so it reads OPS's legal entity. Everything above the fold is you. And if you ever want full ownership, Apple's App Transfer moves the app to your own account — reviews and installs intact — administered by us at billable hours.

faq.items.question: Who owns the code?
faq.items.answer: We do — you hold the license. Here's the line that matters: your app is exclusive to you. We will never sell it or transfer it to another company. The code underneath is ours to reuse — patterns, components, even building a different tool that does a similar job for someone else. Exclusive to you: your instance, your brand, your configuration, your data. Not exclusive: the code and the concept.

faq.items.question: What's the guarantee?
faq.items.answer: Thirty days from delivery to walk away — one invocation, written notice, no defect proof required. On SPEC-01 that means your money back; the automations live in your accounts and stay put. On hosted work we disable the system and refund per the payment schedule. The exclusions are the obvious ones, written plainly in the terms.

faq.items.question: What happens at scope sign-off?
faq.items.answer: Your intake becomes a written work order — what gets built, what counts as accepted, what becomes a change order. You countersign it before build starts, on every tier. On SPEC-03 it's also where your total locks: the $25,000 floor becomes a fixed number, and it doesn't move after that.

faq.items.question: What if I want changes mid-build?
faq.items.answer: Small course corrections happen inside the build. Anything that changes what the scope document promises becomes a change order — priced in writing before we build it. After delivery, the change-hours on your care plan carry the ongoing evolution.

faq.items.question: How fast is this, really?
faq.items.answer: SPEC-01: about a week of build after a few days of discovery. SPEC-02: three to five weeks end to end. SPEC-03: scoped at sign-off — typically six to ten weeks. The board above shows live intake windows; we take a limited number of engagements at a time.

faq.items.question: Can I move up a tier later?
faq.items.answer: Yes. Start where you are. If WORKFLOWS proves the case for a backbone, or your backbone proves the case for your own app, the conversation is short — and where scope overlaps, what you've already paid is credited toward the bigger build.

faq.items.question: Why can't I buy from Quebec?
faq.items.answer: Quebec's consumer-protection rules require French-language contracts and cancellation mechanics we haven't built yet. Rather than serve Quebec badly, we don't serve it yet. The screening happens before any money moves.

faq.items.question: What happens if I stop paying for care?
faq.items.answer: We don't hold your business hostage. Non-payment pauses the system after notice, and off-boarding is defined in the terms: your data exports clean, your brand assets stay yours, and on white-label apps the App Transfer path is always open. What we don't do is keep operating infrastructure for free.

bottomCta.heading: YOUR MOVE.
bottomCta.subtitle: Start with a conversation. We'll tell you which tier is yours — or that you don't need one yet.
bottomCta.defaultOpsText: Not ready for a build? OPS starts free
confirmation.heading: You're in.
confirmation.subtitle: Deposit received. Your intake link is on the way — that's the next thing that matters.
confirmation.timeline.spec03: SPEC-03 delivery is scoped at sign-off — typically 6-10 weeks.
guide.title: Sixty seconds. One straight answer.
guide.branch.prompt: Where are you with OPS today?
guide.branch.cold.label: Haven't tried it yet — I'm just sizing it up
guide.branch.cold.hint: Never logged in. I want to know what I'd need before I start.
guide.branch.existing.label: I'm in OPS right now and something's blocking me
guide.branch.existing.hint: Logged in, using it, and I've hit a wall I can't get past.
guide.c1.prompt: What are you running your business on right now?
guide.c1.manual.label: Texts, paper, whiteboard, spreadsheets
guide.c1.manual.hint: Nothing in a real system yet.
guide.c1.light_tool.label: One or more apps — Jobber, Housecall, QuickBooks, AppFolio, ServiceTitan, anything
guide.c1.light_tool.hint: Whatever you use today, light or heavy.
guide.c2.prompt: Do you need your old history brought across, or are you fine starting clean?
guide.c2.start_clean.label: Fine starting clean — connecting going forward is enough
guide.c2.start_clean.hint: I don't need the old records moved.
guide.c2.bring_history.label: I need my history moved across — I can't lose it
guide.c2.bring_history.hint: Years of jobs, clients, records that have to come over.
guide.c3.solo.label: One trade, one crew
guide.c3.solo.hint: Just me, or a single tight crew.
guide.c3.multi_crew.label: One trade, a few crews
guide.c3.multi_crew.hint: Growing, but still one line of work.
guide.c3.multi_division.label: Multiple trades, divisions, subs, or locations
guide.c3.multi_division.hint: Different divisions, subs to manage, or more than one location.
guide.c4.prompt: How much lands in your inbox or on paper that someone re-types somewhere else?
guide.c4.rarely.label: Rarely — not much double entry here
guide.c4.rarely.hint: Things mostly land where they belong.
guide.c4.weekly.label: Every week — invoices, leads, receipts get re-typed
guide.c4.constant.label: Constantly — half my office time is moving information around
guide.c4.constant.hint: Evenings and Fridays, lost to it.
guide.c5.prompt: Is there a whole tool your trade needs that generic software doesn't have?
guide.c5.no.label: No — the usual flow covers how I work
guide.c5.yes.label: Yes — deck drawings, roof measures, route plans, something built for my trade
guide.c5.yes.hint: A capability, not just a feature.
guide.c6.prompt: Once you know what you need — set it up yourself, or have us do it?
guide.c6.show_me.label: Show me where things are, then I run with it
guide.c6.show_me.hint: A quick walkthrough — fast and free.
guide.c6.myself.label: I'll set it up myself
guide.c6.myself.hint: Me or my admin. Settings are self-serve and free.
guide.c6.not_sure.label: Not sure yet — point me the right way
guide.c6.not_sure.hint: I just know what I have isn't working.
guide.c6.do_for_me.label: Rather you set it up for me, done right
guide.c6.do_for_me.hint: A done-for-you option, if you want it — not required.
guide.e1.setting.label: A setting I couldn't find — colors, follow-up timing, win odds, lead sources, tags, roles, catalog, tax
guide.e1.setting.hint: All self-serve — we'll point you to the exact screen.
guide.e1.stages.label: I need stages my work has that the app doesn't — permits, inspections, approvals
guide.e1.stages.hint: Adding or renaming stages isn't self-serve.
guide.e1.fields.label: I need custom fields on jobs or clients OPS has no box for
guide.e1.data.label: I need old jobs, clients, history brought in from another system
guide.e1.data.hint: We bring it in for you. Scope decides the price.
guide.e1.missing.label: A whole thing OPS just doesn't do — a feature or module that isn't in the app at all
guide.e1.missing.hint: If OPS genuinely can't do it, that's a conversation.
guide.disconfirm.prompt: A lot of "OPS can't do this" turns out to be a setting. Is what you need any of these — auto follow-up reminders, stage colors, win-probability, lead sources, tags, roles?
guide.disconfirm.is_setting.label: Yes — one of those
guide.disconfirm.genuinely_missing.label: No — genuinely not in the app
guide.e2.simple.label: A spreadsheet or one simple tool, one business
guide.e2.incumbent_simple.label: Off a full platform, one business
guide.e2.incumbent_complex.label: Off a full platform, and multiple divisions or branches
guide.e2.incumbent_complex.hint: A real migration across a complex operation.
guide.e3.one_module.label: One thing — the rest of OPS fits how I work
guide.e3.several.label: Several things — or a whole workflow OPS doesn't carry
guide.result.label: // YOUR MOVE
guide.result.ops.headline: OPS
guide.result.ops.reason: Standard OPS already covers you. Start the 30-day free trial — set your pipeline, follow-up timing, lead sources, roles, catalog and tax yourself, in an afternoon. You don't pay us a cent past your subscription.
guide.result.spec01.headline: SPEC-01 · WORKFLOWS
guide.result.spec01.reason: Your tools already know the answers — they're just not talking to each other. We wire your inbox into your ledger, your email into your job folders, your forms into your call list. $2,000 fixed, live in about a week.
guide.result.spec02.headline: SPEC-02 · SYSTEMS
guide.result.spec02.reason: Your operation needs one backbone — jobs, clients, and money in one system, your history moved in, dashboards you'll actually read. $7,500 fixed, run for you.
guide.result.spec03.headline: SPEC-03 · PROPRIETARY
guide.result.spec03.reason: What you're describing is a whole tool, not a feature. That's SPEC-03 territory — a standalone app built only for you. It starts with a conversation, not a checkout.
guide.driver.fits_oob: [ everything you need is already in OPS — you just make it yours ]
guide.driver.guided_self: [ a quick walkthrough and you're running — nothing to build or buy ]
guide.driver.deferred_unsure: [ not sure yet? start free — it's the fastest way to find out ]
guide.driver.re_typing: [ someone's re-typing what your inbox already knows — that's exactly what SPEC-01 kills ]
guide.driver.structure: [ multiple trades, divisions, or locations — that's a backbone problem ]
guide.driver.migration: [ your history has to come with you — we move it and run it ]
guide.driver.setting: [ what's blocking you is a setting — here's where it lives ]
guide.driver.feature_gap: [ stages and fields your work needs — that's SYSTEMS work inside your OPS ]
guide.driver.data_setup: [ you've got history to bring in — that's the $399 Data Setup add-on, not a build ]
guide.driver.trade_tool: [ a whole tool your trade needs — that's a founder conversation ]
guide.driver.several: [ several custom pieces — that's a bigger conversation ]
guide.notes.dataSetupRider: Bringing your history? Once you're in, the Data Setup add-on imports your old jobs, clients and records for you — $399, one-time.
guide.notes.spec01Door: Or skip the wiring and have us do it — SPEC-01 wires your tools for you. $2,000 fixed.
guide.notes.founderEscape: Sounds like your trade needs a tool of its own. Skip the guessing — tell the founder what you're after.
guide.notes.settingPointer: It's a setting, not a wall — check Settings: stage colors, follow-up timing, win odds, lead sources, tags, roles, catalog, tax. All self-serve.
guide.notes.trialLead: No reason to wait — the free trial is open while we talk.
